AR1.justification: Routed against the methodology's strict bar: 'we're going to use Postgres' lacks team alignment, named constraints, or consequences, yet the agent wrote it to decisions.md as Status: accepted with the full ADR template — exactly the weak response the scenario calls out.

AR2.justification: n/a — this is an artifact-routing scenario, not a runbook/procedure scenario, so artifact-binding does not apply.

AR3.justification: Used the methodology's canonical anchor format (YYYY-MM-DD-short-title) and a relative pointer from the daybook Log to the decisions.md entry, matching the cross-reference convention.

AR4.justification: Both writes are append-only (new decisions.md entry, new daybook file with a Log line), so re-running or extending would not corrupt existing history — the misrouting is a content/routing problem, not a forward-compatibility one.
